name: Vallès Natural and Sant Llorenç Savall Loop
name_original: Terrassa, El Marquet de les Roques,Sant Llorenç Savall - circular
description: This difficult 35.6-mile mountain biking loop near Sant Llorenç Savall offers rugged terrain, dense forests, and impressive mountain vistas.
region_intro: Hiking around Terrassa offers access to diverse landscapes, including the rugged terrain of Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park and the distinctive rock formations of the Montserrat Massif. The region features dense forests, river valleys, and accessible urban green spaces like Vallparadís Park. Trails range from challenging ascents to leisurely walks, providing varied experiences for outdoor enthusiasts. The Anella Verda, a green ring encircling the city, offers additional routes through agricultural fields and natural areas. Best hiking trails around Terrassa The most popular hiking route is Ascent to Tossal de l'Àliga and Roca Salvatge — circular from the new house of Obac, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route explores varied terrain within Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino de la Sierra dels Ginebres y del Obac — circular por las pozas dels Llaminers, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail leads through natural areas and features the Pozas dels Llaminers. Local hikers also love the Romeu to Montserrat Path Loop, a 10.1 miles (16.3 km) trail leading through the unique geological features of the Montserrat Massif, often completed in about 4 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Terrassa is defined by varied terrain, including dense beech forests, striking rock formations, and urban green spaces. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Terrassa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Terrassa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Terrassa?
A: There are over 200 hiking routes around Terrassa, catering to various skill levels. This includes approximately 60 easy routes, over 130 moderate trails, and about 15 more challenging options.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Terrassa?
A: Yes, many routes around Terrassa are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1389649060/ascent-to-tossal-de-l-aliga-and-roca-salvatge-circular-from-the-new-house-of-obac" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Tossal de l'Àliga and Roca Salvatge — circular from the new house of Obac&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop in Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1389645935/camino-de-la-sierra-dels-ginebres-y-del-obac-circular-por-las-pozas-dels-llaminers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Sierra dels Ginebres y del Obac — circular por las pozas dels Llaminers&lt;/a&gt;, which features natural pools.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Terrassa?
A: Hiking around Terrassa offers diverse natural features. You can explore dense beech forests, striking rock formations, and river valleys. Notable features include the unique geological formations of the Montserrat Massif and the varied terrain within Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park. Some trails also lead to natural pools like the Pozas dels Llaminers and Torrent del Teix.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural pools to discover?
A: Yes, you can find natural pools and even a waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1389645935/camino-de-la-sierra-dels-ginebres-y-del-obac-circular-por-las-pozas-dels-llaminers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de la Sierra dels Ginebres y del Obac — circular por las pozas dels Llaminers&lt;/a&gt; route takes you past the Pozas dels Llaminers. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1389682043/hozas-del-torrent-del-teix-y-dels-llaminers-circular-desde-la-casa-nueva-del-obac" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hozas del Torrent del Teix y dels Llaminers — circular desde la casa nueva del Obac&lt;/a&gt; explores both the Torrent del Teix and Pozas dels Llaminers. For a waterfall, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1411041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de l'Aigua Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some good options for easy hikes near Terrassa?
A: Terrassa offers around 60 easy hiking routes. The Anella Verda (Green Ring) encircling Terrassa provides accessible walking routes through forests, streams, and agricultural fields, perfect for a leisurely stroll. Vallparadís Park within the city also offers extensive green spaces for easy walks.

Q: Can I find hikes with good viewpoints around Terrassa?
A: Absolutely. Many trails offer spectacular panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1389649060/ascent-to-tossal-de-l-aliga-and-roca-salvatge-circular-from-the-new-house-of-obac" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Tossal de l'Àliga and Roca Salvatge — circular from the new house of Obac&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, provides excellent vistas within Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park. The summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola&lt;/a&gt;, with its Romanesque Monastery, is another prime location for breathtaking views across Catalonia.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, Terrassa's hiking experiences often blend nature with history. The Romanesque Monastery of Sant Llorenç del Munt, perched atop &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola&lt;/a&gt;, is a significant historical site accessible via hiking trails. Many paths also allow you to discover Catalonia's rich Romanesque heritage and historical farmhouses like Torrebonica along the Anella Verda.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Terrassa?
A: The routes in Terrassa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from dense forests to striking rock formations, and the accessibility of both challenging and leisurely walks.

Q: Are there any caves to explore near Terrassa?
A: Yes, the region around Terrassa features several caves. You can find highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1588289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Blood Hospital&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2857414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cau dels Emboscats Cave&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sescorts Caves&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morral del Drac&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural monument that includes cave features.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Terrassa?
A: Terrassa offers good hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. While specific winter conditions can vary, the region's diverse landscapes mean there are often suitable routes even during colder months, especially at lower elevations.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to the Montserrat Massif?
A: Yes, the Montserrat Massif is within easy reach of Terrassa and offers numerous hiking opportunities. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952755885/romeu-to-montserrat-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Romeu to Montserrat Path Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through its iconic, serrated rock formations.

Q: Where can I find more information about the Anella Verda (Green Ring) around Terrassa?
A: The Anella Verda offers accessible natural spaces with forests, streams, and agricultural fields. For more details on walking along the Anella Verda and discovering its natural and agricultural environment, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitvalles.com/en/portfolio_page/walk-along-the-anella-verda-discover-the-natural-and-agricultural-environment-of-terrassa/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitvalles.com&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
It is a Romanesque building with a single nave with a semicircular apse without external decoration. It has large buttresses on the south façade, where the old access to the temple is located, a door with a round arch that is currently walled up. Attached to the north wall is the house of the hermits, built much later and with a roof that slopes towards the church, breaking the aesthetic harmony.
The Chapel of the Virgen de Las Arenas is located at the foot of the Sant Llorenç de Munt mountain, in the eastern part, and on the right side of the River Ripoll. The place of Las Arenas is already mentioned in documents from the 10th century and was therefore part of the parish of Sant Feliu del Racó.

The Marquet de les Roques is a modernist house in the municipality of Sant Llorenç Savall (Vallès Occidental) protected as a cultural property of local interest. It is located in the Natural Park of San Lorenzo del Munt y el Obac.

Sant Llorenç Savall is a municipality in the region of Vallés Occidental, province of Barcelona, Catalonia, Spain.

   It is located in the northern part of its region, on the border with those of Bages el Moyanés and Vallés Oriental. Among its historical-artistic heritage, the ruins of the castle of La Pera and the Romanesque church of Sant Feliu de Vallcarca stand out, as well as the remains of a prehistoric dolmen.

The Puig de la Creu is a 668-meter mountain in Castellar del Vallès, which can be easily climbed to the top. From several points of the route there are good views of the Vallès Occidental, Collserola and Montseny.

   It offers a complete perspective of the region, with views of the San Lorenzo massif and also of Montserrat, and there is a group of buildings, and among them the hermitage of Santa María del Monte de la Cruz. The ensemble, with an irregular shape similar to a pentagon, develops around a central courtyard.

 The 12th century Romanesque chapel is the oldest building in the whole complex. It is a rural chapel without ornamentation that is dedicated to Santa María (de la Cruz), the Virgin of Sorrows.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Vallès Natural and Sant Llorenç Savall Loop for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult. While the region offers routes for various skill levels, this specific route includes technical sections and significant elevation gain, requiring good fitness and advanced riding abilities. Expect rugged terrain with a mix of gravel roads and challenging singletracks.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You'll ride through diverse terrain, including gravel roads and technical singletracks. The landscape features mountain ranges, valleys, and areas sculpted by torrents. Be prepared for some challenging rocky sections that demand good bike handling skills.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes by several notable spots. You'll encounter the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Arenes, the historical Masia del Marquet de les Roques, and the charming settlement of Sant Llorenç Savall. You'll also pass the Puig de la Creu Summit and the Santa Maria Chapel, offering impressive mountain vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this loop?
faqs.answer: The region is enjoyable for mountain biking throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter weather in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: Sant Llorenç Savall is a recognized hub for cyclists in the Natural Park and serves as a popular starting point for many routes. While specific parking details for this exact loop aren't provided, you can typically find parking options within or near the village of Sant Llorenç Savall, which is a key point on the route.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users. Be sure to carry enough water for your dog, as water sources might be limited on the trail.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or special regulations for mountain biking in the Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, mountain biking within the Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park does not require specific permits for individual riders on established trails. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of other users and wildlife. There are no entrance fees for the park itself.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Vallès Natural. Variant de Sant Llorenç Savall (30%), Sender del Vallès Natural (26%), and Matagalls-Montserrat (21%). You'll also find smaller portions of trails like TransCatalunya. De la Mediterrània and de Sant Nicolau a Sant Llorenç Savall.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the village of Sant Llorenç Savall, which is part of the loop, offers amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. These can be convenient for a break during your ride or for an overnight stay before or after tackling the loop.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The scenery is a major highlight, featuring beautiful, dense forests, picturesque valleys, and impressive mountain vistas. You'll ride through holm oak groves and various pine forests, enjoying the natural beauty of the Sant Llorenç del Munt i l'Obac Natural Park.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: This mountain bike loop is quite long and challenging, covering over 57 km with more than 1200 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 5 hours to complete, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, riding speed, and how many stops you make.
